title: Gently Spiced Middle Eastern Lamb Shawarma
description: A tangy and tender Middle Eastern dish featuring lamb, served in a low-carb friendly shawarma format with flat rice noodles, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, cumin, coriander, paprika, garlic, salt, and pepper to make the marinade.
Add the sliced lamb to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and cook the lamb for 5-6 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
Immediately submerge the cooked lamb in an ice bath to stop the cooking process and slice it into thin strips.
In a small bowl, mix together yogurt and lemon juice to make the sauce.
To assemble the shawarma, place a portion of the cooked flat rice noodles on a plate, then add sliced lamb and drizzle with the yogurt sauce.
Garnish with additional lemon wedges and parsley if desired.
